Now we're getting into the practical application of understanding what standards mean, the impact they'll have on your life, either positive or negative, and what you do about it. And you're learning about standards and you say, you know what, this makes, I'm that guy, I'm that girl, right? I never thought I could make this much money. They know they're capable of more. What do you do? You had all the questions and graphics, so you know it very well, right? Now that you understand what you're really capable of, I think almost at the beginning, there's like disbelief. I mean, what's the point of raising my standards when it's fictitious? Like, I can't achieve that. But then as you go through the straight line, you're like, oh my God, like I have skills I never thought I have. So wait a second. So you mean my past doesn't really equal my future? No, it doesn't. That's like a buzzword in like self-drawn. Your past doesn't equal your future. You probably heard that somewhere, right? And mostly it's non-sane because usually your past does equal your future if you don't do things to make yourself stronger, to grow. You have to change. In other words, your past, your past results are your past results for a number of reasons. One of them is because if you didn't bring the right skills from table, the right belief systems from table, yes, your past doesn't equal your future if you're willing to do things to educate yourself, to improve yourself, to change some belief systems, and of course to raise your standard, right? So this notion, where does it start? Well, it starts just, let's talk about an individual first. What would you do? Let's say you want to raise your own standards. It starts with getting honest. What's my standard at? Where is my standard? Like, it's like, I hate to say it, but it's almost like a little bit of alcohol synonymous. You know, it's like, you know, first step is getting honest. Oh, he's like, where really am I? What is my, do I have a problem? Am I powerless over drugs and alcohol? Am I powerless over my own success because my standard's too low on some level? In other words, how do you know if your standard's too low, right? That's the obvious pressure. Well, here's a gut check, right? What happens is if your standard is very low, but you have a bold vision for your future, what typically happens, you have all these ideas and you talk about taking action and you think about it and you even take some action, but for some reason, you seem to not follow through. You back off and it often manifests itself in an inability to get yourself to do the things that you know you have to do even when you don't want to do them. In other words, people can get themselves to do things they love to do. That's easy. It's, what about things that you hate doing? See, there's things that I hate to do and you've seen me do is I can manufacture such power and focus on things that are miserably boring because I know I have to do them to be successful, right? That's a result of my standard because I can't accept less. I can't accept less in my life. So that's the first question is, you know, hey, do I need to raise my standard? Where is my standard at right now? Well, again, many people have this incongruity and I call it, I mentioned a thing in the intro of having, you know, you have this, I don't know, maybe I didn't know, you're having a beer standard and a champagne vision, which means that your champagne vision is, you see yourself on a yacht with a champagne and beautiful girls of men or whatever, you have the wealth and success. That's your vision, but your standard. Will you really do what's necessary? Do you have that whatever it takes mentality and whatever it takes ability to harness that much personal power and focus versus state grade? If you don't, well, it's either one of two things. Number one, sometimes because you don't have your why identified, you don't know why the vision matters to you. And that's important as well. And we discussed that in the vision module. But now this is the other side is that what about the fact that maybe you just don't have that demand on yourself? It's not a must. It's not an absolute must for you. The way you look at your life, what you will not settle for less than is not congruent with your vision. So your vision is this, but I'll be okay without that. I won't be okay without my vision. I'll be okay, but I don't feel okay inside. And I almost play on that and allow myself to see me into that negativity to make sure I don't get complacent. So it starts right there. So if you have this great vision for your future and hopefully everyone that's on this train, you should have that. If I ain't done that, right? The question really is, is what is your standard? Is it serving you? Do you need to raise it? Do you find yourself not able to get yourself to do the things you have to do? Is that a problem for you? Has it always been a problem? Or how about this? Are there some areas in your life where you find yourself following through very easily, yet when it comes to money and success, you don't follow through? See I would almost look to areas where you say that's like that, oh my God, I follow through that. Well, that's your high standard. And that shows it's possible. You can be a follow through person, right, in some areas of your life. We have people, for instance, that are broke, but you look at that picture and it's going to be like they're ripped, they're in the best shape. They have a really high standard for their body and how they eat. Very low standard for money, right? You see other people who have incredibly high standards for money and their body, but their relationships are a mess because they have low relationship standards, right? Even standards with God that goes to everything. Well, nature, whatever you believe in, right? So that first step is honesty. Identify, okay, do I have a problem? Because hey, you know, listen, I don't need to raise my standard. I really don't. I would never, I want to waste my time, okay? I would never waste my time because my standard is really, really high. In fact, my standard serves me, right? However, that being said, I have had times where my vision wasn't clear enough, right? I found myself moving through life and I'm always a very hard worker. It happens to me I have a very high standard, but you haven't really taken the time of let's say your vision's outdated, no longer serving you, right? Well, you know what? That standard will drive you to hard work and excellence, but you don't feel fulfilled. You don't kind of jump up out of bed every day and feel like you want to seize the world. I can't remember any time in my life more than right now where I literally jumped out of bed in the morning at 4.45, 5.00 am the latest and I am working and I don't consider it worked. To me, it's just, it's thought it's amazing because that's congruency now between a standard and a vision. So that's step number one is you got to get honest with yourself. You can write things down, make an inventory of your activities and really try to get honest.
